prompt: I'm building a Node.js blog app with SQLite and better-sqlite3. Can you act as a database engineer and audit my DB layer? I think there might be some missing indexes and slow queries but I'm not sure where.

assertions.description: Identifies the N+1 query pattern — loop in GET / fetching comments and author per post individually
assertions.check: Report mentions N+1 or loop+query pattern in the GET / posts route

assertions.description: Reports at least 3 of the 6 missing SQLite PRAGMAs (journal_mode, synchronous, foreign_keys, cache_size, temp_store, mmap_size)
assertions.check: Report mentions PRAGMA and at least 3 specific PRAGMA names

assertions.description: Flags unindexed foreign key columns: posts.user_id, comments.post_id, comments.user_id, likes.post_id
assertions.check: Report mentions missing indexes on at least 2 FK columns

assertions.description: Flags the /search route has no LIMIT — could return thousands of rows
assertions.check: Report flags missing LIMIT or unbounded result in search query

assertions.description: Flags DELETE route performs 3 writes (likes, comments, posts) without a transaction
assertions.check: Report mentions missing transaction on the DELETE /:id route

assertions.description: Flags /stats pulls all posts into memory to count by status instead of using SQL COUNT
assertions.check: Report flags the app-side aggregation pattern in /stats

assertions.description: Report is structured with severity tiers (Critical / Important / Nice-to-have or equivalent)
assertions.check: Report contains at least two distinct severity levels for findings

assertions.description: Report does not modify any source files — only produces an audit report
assertions.check: No changes were written to db.js or routes/posts.js

assertions.description: Ends by asking the user to approve before applying fixes
assertions.check: Report ends with a question or prompt asking user to confirm before changes are made
